reasoning: The document exhibits numerous major defects across both LLM fidelity and programmatic evaluations. Key issues include garbled text due to merged strikethrough/added text in rule amendments, flattened nested lists, duplicated content via full-page images alongside extracted text, significant retention of headers/footers (255 instances), structural table issues, displaced hyperlinks, and incomplete alt text on 59 images. With well over 5 major defects, the count-based routing rules dictate a REJECT.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: In the sections detailing rule amendments (e.g., Tarpon rules), the source uses strikethrough text for deletions and underlined text for additions. The extraction pipeline read both the deleted and added text sequentially without any formatting or indication of the deletion, resulting in confusing, garbled text that alters the literal reading of the document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: (a) [strikethrough: It is unlawful to](1) sell or offer for sale [strikethrough: tarpon.]tarpon;
concerns.output_quote: (a) It is unlawful to(1) sell or offer for sale tarpon.tarpon;

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly
concerns.analysis: There are instances where nested lists are flattened and numbering is lost. On page 211, under 'Option 2' and 'Option 3', the source PDF contains sub-points numbered '(3)' and '(4)' ('(3) spear, gaff, or puncture a tarpon.' and '(4) spear, gaff, or puncture a tarpon.') that are rendered at the same hierarchical level as the parent items, and the numbers '(3)' and '(4)' are completely stripped from the text. Additionally, on page 239, sub-items '(A)' and '(B)' under item '(a)(2)' are incorrectly
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
(c) It is unlawful to(2) take tarpon by any method other than hook and line.hook and line; and
(3) spear, gaff, or puncture a tarpon.
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "(c) It is unlawful to(2) take tarpon by any method other than hook and line.hook and line; and"}, {"text": "spear, gaff, or puncture a tarpon."}

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains instances of full-page images or cropped page sections embedded alongside the extracted text of those same pages. This is most prominent in Appendix A (a scanned letter from the Department of Commerce), where the text of the letter is fully transcribed in HTML, but multiple images of the scanned letter itself are also inserted into the document flow. This forces the user to see the same content twice: once as an image and once as text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 203 shows a scanned letter from the United States Department of Commerce dated JUL 19 2017.
concerns.output_quote:
UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration NATIONAL MARINE FISHERIES SERVICE Silver Spring, MD 20910

JUL 19 2017

...

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 5 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=5. Examples: pages 126-127: 3 cols (year, date(s), regulation change...); pages 127-128: 3 cols (year, date(s), regulation change...); pages 131-132: 4 cols (season, date, violation code...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 15 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=15. Examples: p57: "January"; p57: "February"; p57: "March". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Data row marked as header: 2 table(s) have numeric/data values in the header row
concerns.analysis: data_as_header=2. Examples: p205: [2009-2011, 2013-2015]; p205: [2009-2011, 2013-2015]. Header rows should contain column labels, not data values.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (3 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=3. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 145 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=145, examples=['http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/02-2020-briefing-book', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (255): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=255, pages_affected=245/248. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 19 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: [youtube.com; [portal.ncdenr.org; https://www.ncsbe.gov/Ethics/Education; https://EFILE.ncsbe.gov/; (http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/proclamation-m-19-2017).
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=19. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 9 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=9. Examples: p42: [portal.ncdenr.org; p212: Tina.Moore@ncdenr.gov; p212: Tina.Moore@ncdenr.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "***Times indicated are merely for guidance. The co". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p62→p63: list item ends “…eading of nesting habitat) of this long-”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Rule Amendments:"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 1 form(s) with 2 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=1, total_fields=2. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 59 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (57 truncated mid-sentence, 2 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...r sunset, featuring calm water, marsh grass, and a"; p17: "...nd and a white header displaying the word "LETTERS"; p29: "...lock of black redaction covering text in the upper"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=59 (truncated=57, placeholder=2, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 38 link(s) clustered at bottom of 14 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=14, total_displaced=38. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Visual emphasis loss
concerns.analysis: The source document heavily uses underline formatting to denote new additions to legal rules (e.g., in the recreational water quality rule amendments). Because the extraction format does not support markdown for underlines, this emphasis is completely lost in the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The sign for waters impacted by a wastewater spill shall state the following: [Underlined text follows]
concerns.output_quote: The sign for waters impacted by a wastewater spill shall state the following:

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "may 14, 2020"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "march 18, 2020"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "or 252-808-8022."
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "9:00 a.m."; 18 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=18. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 42 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 6: "http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/02-2020-briefing-b"; page 11: "http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_fi"; page 11: "e=DLFE-142450.pdf"; page 13: "e=DLFE-142457.pdf"; page 13: "e=DLFE-142458.pdf"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=42. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
